quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 4 Quiz 4
quiz.subtitle: Conservation of Momentum — Solving for Mass, Initial Velocity, and Final Velocity
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A $3\text{ kg}$ cart moving at $4\text{ m/s}$ collides with a $2\text{ kg}$ cart at rest. After the collision, they stick together. What is their final velocity?

A $2\text{ kg}$ ball moving at $6\text{ m/s}$ collides with a $4\text{ kg}$ ball at rest. The $2\text{ kg}$ ball rebounds at $2\text{ m/s}$ backward. What is the final velocity of the $4\text{ kg}$ ball?
A $5\text{ kg}$ cart moving at $3\text{ m/s}$ collides with a stationary cart. They stick together and move at $1.2\text{ m/s}$. What is the mass of the second cart?
A $1,500\text{ kg}$ car rear-ends a $1,000\text{ kg}$ car at rest. They lock together and move at $9\text{ m/s}$. What was the initial speed of the first car?

A $10\text{ kg}$ object moving at $5\text{ m/s}$ collides with a $15\text{ kg}$ object moving at $2\text{ m/s}$ in the same direction. After the collision, the $15\text{ kg}$ object moves at $4\text{ m/s}$. What is the final velocity of the $10\text{ kg}$ object?
A stationary cannon fires a $0.5\text{ kg}$ cannonball forward at $120\text{ m/s}$. The cannon recoils backward at $5\text{ m/s}$. What is the mass of the cannon?

Two carts roll toward each other. Cart A ($4\text{ kg}$) moves right at $6\text{ m/s}$. Cart B ($2\text{ kg}$) moves left at an unknown speed. They collide and both stop completely. What was the initial speed of Cart B?
questions.explanation: $(4 \times 6) + (2 \times u_B) = 0$. $24 + 2u_B = 0$. $2u_B = -24$. $u_B = -12\text{ m/s}$. Speed $= 12\text{ m/s}$ to the left.

questions.question: A $50\text{ kg}$ ice skater at rest throws a $3\text{ kg}$ ball forward at $10\text{ m/s}$. What is the skater's velocity after the throw?

A $6\text{ kg}$ object moving at $8\text{ m/s}$ collides with a stationary object. After the collision, the $6\text{ kg}$ object moves at $2\text{ m/s}$ forward, and the second object moves at $9\text{ m/s}$ forward. What is the mass of the second object?
A $3\text{ kg}$ cart moving right at $5\text{ m/s}$ collides with a $2\text{ kg}$ cart moving left at $3\text{ m/s}$. They stick together. What is their final velocity?
A $1,200\text{ kg}$ car skids into a $900\text{ kg}$ parked car. The two cars lock and slide together at $6\text{ m/s}$ after impact. What was the speed of the first car before the collision?
A $5\text{ kg}$ object moving east at $8\text{ m/s}$ collides with a $3\text{ kg}$ object moving west at $4\text{ m/s}$. They stick together. What is their final velocity?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered conservation of momentum calculations!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review problems involving opposite directions — remember to assign positive and negative signs.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on setting up $m_1u_1 + m_2u_2 = m_1v_1 + m_2v_2$ correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: conservation of momentum, sign conventions for direction, and solving for the unknown variable.
